mission.title: traefik: Encrypt Session Sticky cookie content
mission.description: Encrypt Session Sticky cookie content. Requested by 47+ users.

steps.title: Check current traefik deployment
steps.description:
Verify your traefik version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik
helm list -n traefik 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working traefik installation.

steps.title: Review traefik configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant traefik configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik
kubectl get configmap -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=traefik
```
### Do you want to request a *feature* or report a *bug*?
request a feature

### What did you do?
I enabled session sticky under docker.

### What did you expect to see?

The Traefik session sticky cookie content to be encrypted text.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Encrypt Session Sticky cookie content
steps.description:
Hi ,

In your PR on oxy, your changes are breaking due to the changes made on the cookie value.
Could you add an option in oxy to enable/disable the hashing?

We would also suggest that adding a consistent hashing load balancing algorithm (#1139) could be a great workaround. If anyone is interested
```yaml
<!--
Add more configuration information here.
-->


### If applicable, please paste the log output in debug mode (`--debug` switch)
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik
kubectl get events -n traefik --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Encrypt Session Sticky cookie content" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Hi ,

In your PR on oxy, your changes are breaking due to the changes made on the cookie value.
Could you add an option in oxy to enable/disable the hashing?

We would also suggest that adding a consistent hashing load balancing algorithm (#1139) could be a great workaround. If anyone is interested to work on this, we could definitely help :)

Thanks!

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with traefik installed or the issue environment reproducible.
